description: Fix four categories of flaky test patterns in E2E tests that cause spurious CI failures without the product being broken. After all stories pass: (1) side-panel-instant-startup's ready-state detection no longer passes trivially when the loading indicator is absent from DOM; (2) group-header count assertions use Playwright's retrying toHaveCount instead of non-retrying count(); (3) multi-connection's fixed 500ms sleep before dispatch is replaced with an observable signal; (4) dispatch-resilience's 29s upper timing bound is widened to safely accommodate the 30s server dispatch timeout.

userStories.title: Fix toBeHidden on zero-match locator in side-panel-instant-startup
userStories.description: In side-panel-instant-startup.e2e.ts, the test uses `await expect(e2ePluginCard.locator('[class*="border-border/30"]')).toBeHidden({ timeout: 30_000 })` as a proxy for 'plugin tab is ready'. Playwright's `toBeHidden()` returns true both when an element exists and is hidden AND when zero elements match the selector. If the plugin card renders without the `border-border/30` loading indicator at all (e.g., the card skips the loading state and goes directly to ready), the assertion passes immediately without any wait — the test never actually confirms the ready state. The assertion appears at four points in the file (~lines 110, 127, 194, 213).
acceptanceCriteria: At all four assertion sites in e2e/side-panel-instant-startup.e2e.ts, the ready-state check first asserts the plugin card is visible, then uses a retrying locator pattern that correctly distinguishes 'not rendered yet' from 'rendered and ready'
The assertion no longer passes trivially when zero elements match `[class*="border-border/30"]`
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-instant-startup.e2e.ts (fix 4 occurrences of toBeHidden on zero-match locator)

Context:
The current pattern:
```ts
const e2ePluginCard = sidePanelPage.locator('button[aria-expanded]').filter({ hasText: 'E2E Test' });
await expect(e2ePluginCard.locator('[class*="border-border/30"]')).toBeHidden({ timeout: 30_000 });
```
The `border-border/30` class is presumably added to the plugin card's visual indicator ONLY when the tab state is loading/unavailable (faded border). When the tab is 'ready', this indicator is either hidden or removed. But if the card renders without this class at all (zero elements), `toBeHidden()` passes trivially.

Fix options:
1. Change the assertion to first verify the card IS in the DOM and then verify the loading indicator is specifically NOT visible by scoping to a known parent that always exists:
```ts
await expect(e2ePluginCard).toBeVisible({ timeout: 30_000 });
await expect(e2ePluginCard.locator('[class*="border-border/30"]')).toHaveCount(0);
```
`toHaveCount(0)` auto-retries and will fail fast if an element appears. However, this is the same issue — it passes if the locator never matched.

2. Better fix: use a positive assertion for the READY state instead of a negative assertion for the NOT-LOADING state. Read the side panel component source to find the CSS class that indicates the plugin is 'ready' (e.g., a solid border or a green indicator) and assert that it IS visible:
```ts
await expect(e2ePluginCard.locator('[class*="border-primary"]')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 30_000 });
```

Read e2e/side-panel-instant-startup.e2e.ts to understand the full context. Also read platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (or similar) to understand what CSS classes indicate the 'ready' vs 'loading' state so you can write a positive assertion.

Constraints:
- The test is about 'instant startup' — it verifies the side panel renders quickly after connect. The ready-state check should use a positive assertion for the ready state, not a negative assertion for the absence of a loading class.

userStories.title: Replace non-retrying count() with toHaveCount in group-header tests
userStories.description: In side-panel-plugin-tool-groups.e2e.ts and side-panel-browser-tool-groups.e2e.ts, the tests verify that group headers render correctly by first awaiting `groupHeaders.first()` to be visible, then calling `groupHeaders.count()` without retry. Playwright's `count()` reads the DOM at the instant it's called without auto-retry — if React is still appending subsequent group header elements at that moment, the count will be lower than expected and the assertion fails spuriously. The correct pattern is `await expect(groupHeaders).toHaveCount(n)` which auto-retries.
acceptanceCriteria: In e2e/side-panel-plugin-tool-groups.e2e.ts, the `await groupHeaders.count()` call followed by `expect(count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(n)` is replaced with `await expect(groupHeaders).toHaveCount(n)` or `await expect(groupHeaders.nth(n-1)).toBeVisible()`
In e2e/side-panel-browser-tool-groups.e2e.ts, the same non-retrying count pattern is fixed

The filtered-group count assertion in browser-tool-groups.e2e.ts (~line 155-157) uses `await expect(groupHeaders).toHaveCount(1)` instead of `const filteredCount = await groupHeaders.count(); expect(filteredCount).toBe(1)`
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-tool-groups.e2e.ts (fix non-retrying count at ~lines 71-72)
- e2e/side-panel-browser-tool-groups.e2e.ts (fix non-retrying count at ~lines 81-82 and 155-157)

Context:
The pattern in plugin-tool-groups.e2e.ts:
```ts
await expect(groupHeaders.first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });
const headerCount = await groupHeaders.count();
expect(headerCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);
```
The fix:
```ts
await expect(groupHeaders).toHaveCount(3);  // or toHaveCount(n) where n is the known expected count
// OR if the count is dynamic (at least n):
await expect(groupHeaders.nth(1)).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });  // wait for at least 2
```

For the e2e-test plugin with groups 'Basic', 'Data', and 'Other', the count should be exactly 3. Check the expected group count by looking at the e2e-test plugin's tools.json to count distinct groups.

For browser-tool-groups.e2e.ts line ~155-157 (filtered group count):
```ts
const filteredCount = await groupHeaders.count();
expect(filteredCount).toBe(1);
```
Fix:
```ts
await expect(groupHeaders).toHaveCount(1);
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any of the specific `toBeVisible` checks for 'Basic', 'Data', 'Other' — those are already correct
- Only fix the `count()` calls

userStories.title: Replace fixed 500ms sleep in multi-connection dispatch-before-disconnect test
userStories.description: In multi-connection.e2e.ts, the test for 'dispatch while connection closes mid-execution' fires a tool dispatch to wsAlpha and then waits 500ms (hardcoded `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500))`) before closing the connection, assuming the dispatch arrived at wsAlpha within that window. Under heavy CI load, 500ms may elapse before the server has forwarded the tool.dispatch message to wsAlpha, meaning the test closes the connection before the dispatch ever arrives — the pending-dispatch scenario is never exercised.
acceptanceCriteria: The 500ms fixed sleep in the 'dispatch while connection closes' test in e2e/multi-connection.e2e.ts is replaced with a wait that confirms the dispatch message actually arrived at wsAlpha before closing the connection
The test reliably exercises the scenario where the dispatch is in-flight when the connection closes

userStories.title: Widen upper timing bound in dispatch-resilience extension-timeout test
userStories.description: In dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts, the extension-timeout test asserts `expect(elapsed).toBeLessThan(29_000)`. The extension fires its timeout at ~25 seconds (SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS) and the server's dispatch timeout is 30 seconds (DISPATCH_TIMEOUT_MS). The 29s upper bound leaves only 1 second of margin below the server's 30s timeout. On slow CI machines with scheduling jitter, this assertion flakes — the elapsed time legitimately exceeds 29s when the server's 30s timeout fires instead of the extension's 25s timeout.
acceptanceCriteria: The upper timing bound assertion in the extension-timeout test in e2e/dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts is widened to give at least 5 seconds of margin above the expected 25s timeout (i.e., `toBeLessThan(32_000)` or similar)
The lower bound assertion (`toBeGreaterThan(20_000)`) remains unchanged to still verify the timeout actually took some time
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts (widen upper bound assertion at ~lines 413-417)

Context:
The current assertions:
```ts
expect(elapsed).toBeGreaterThan(20_000);
expect(elapsed).toBeLessThan(29_000);
```
The comment says the tool should timeout at ~25s (extension timeout) not 30s (server timeout). The 29s bound is too tight:
- If CI scheduling delays push past 29s, the test fails even when behavior is correct
- The server timeout is 30s, so anything under 30s is still 'extension timed out first'

Fix:
```ts
expect(elapsed).toBeGreaterThan(20_000);
expect(elapsed).toBeLessThan(32_000);  // 2s margin above 30s server timeout
```
Or better, since the intent is 'extension timed out before server':
```ts
expect(elapsed).toBeGreaterThan(20_000);
// The extension timeout fires before the server's 30s dispatch timeout
expect(elapsed).toBeLessThan(35_000);
```
The key invariant is: elapsed should be significantly less than 60s (the test's overall timeout) and greater than 20s. The exact upper bound is less important than catching cases where the timeout never fires at all.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the lower bound (toBeGreaterThan(20_000))
- The comment explaining why the upper bound exists should be updated to reflect the new value
